description_long: This is a rectangular, vintage-style glass bottle with a dark amber hue and a slightly reflective surface. The bottle features a wide, short neck sealed with a cylindrical cork stopper, giving it an old-fashioned apothecary appearance. The front face displays a large, off-white label with black text, including the word 'TONIC' and additional product information, while the other sides are mostly bare except for some distressed, patchy areas of lighter color and greenish stains. The glass surface appears to have a mottled, weathered texture, suggesting age or intentional design for a rustic look. The bottle is compact and sturdy, likely designed to hold a liquid such as lotion or tonic. Its overall aesthetic is both utilitarian and decorative, making it suitable for display or practical use in an urban environment.
description: A vintage-style, rectangular amber glass bottle with a cork stopper and a large front label reading 'TONIC'. The bottle has a weathered, mottled surface and distressed graphic elements.
description_view_0: Front view shows the main label with 'TONIC' branding and product details, as well as the cork stopper.
description_view_1: Side view reveals a mostly bare surface with a patch of off-white and greenish stains, no label text visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the mottled amber glass with no label, highlighting the weathered texture and greenish patches.
description_view_3: Opposite side view shows another off-white patch and mottled glass, similar to view 1 but with different stain patterns.
